title: Use sprintf to generate port numbers for commit
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Use sprintf to properly format the ports integer to a string value.  This mimics the work done by @mheon in Podman at https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2067

comments.body: It's always the quick ones that bite me.  The ExposedPorts (p) field appears to already be a String at least according to the Travis CI checking.  Strangely the code compiles and seems to run find in my sandbox.  Will have to poke more tomorrow when hopefully my eyes aren't as crossed.  If anyone spots some silliness on my part, please feel free to point it out.

OK, I'm going to close this PR and leave the file as is.  The config.ExposedPorts in Buildah is happy with a string while the config.PortMappings.ContainerPort, an int, is not happy dealing with a string.  I'm still not sure why this compiled/ran just fine in my sandbox, but I'm done digging on that.
